items.quote: I thank Marc Jacobs so much for giving me the opportunity to design a shoe for Louis Vuitton, but the thing that broke my heart most was when they said, 'You're finished. The shoe's finished.'
items.author: Kanye West

items.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
items.abstract: This paper introduces **ScaleLogic**, a synthetic framework to systematically study how Reinforcement Learning (RL) improves LLM reasoning across varying **depth (horizon)** and **logical expressiveness**. The core contribution is demonstrating that the required RL training compute scales with reasoning depth via a power law ($T \propto D^γ$), where the scaling exponent ($\gamma$) increases significantly as the underlying logic becomes more expressive (e.g., adding "and," "or," "not," and "for all").
items.figure_caption: Overview of ScaleLogic . Each problem has B B candidate proof trees, exactly one of which has a provable conclusion; the others are made unprovable by corrupting one axiom. The depth D D controls proof depth. Left: Implication-only reasoning. Right: The most expressive logic setting (referred to as + Quantification in Section 3.2 ) combines conjunction, disjunction, negation, and universal quantification.

items.title: Continuous Latent Diffusion Language Model
items.abstract: This paper introduces Cola DLM, a hierarchical latent diffusion language model that separates global semantic organization from local textual realization. It uses a Text VAE for text-to-latent mapping and a block-causal DiT to model a global semantic prior in continuous latent space. This non-autoregressive approach improves generation efficiency and semantic modeling by performing latent prior transport during the diffusion process.
items.figure_caption: The Overall Workflow of Cola DLM. Detailed illustration of the training and inference pipeline of 𝒞 ​ o ​ l ​ a \( \mathcal{C} \)ola DLM . Training Stage 1 shows Text VAE pretraining with reconstruction, BERT, and KL losses. Training Stage 2 shows joint pretraining of the Text VAE and Text DiT with gradient control for stable optimization, where a specialized block-causal mechanism is adopted in the DiT. Inference Stage illustrates the decoding process with KV cache.

items.title: Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors
items.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, low-stakes benchmark to measure the propensity of LLM agents to engage in instrumental convergence (IC) behaviors, such as self-preservation, which might lead to instruction violation. The benchmark comprises seven tasks, each featuring a policy-violating shortcut, and an accompanying framework to test how varying factors influence this behavior. The core contribution is a realistic evaluation method to quantify the risk of AI agents pursuing useful but unintended instrumental goals.
items.figure_caption: Aggregate adjusted instrumental-convergence (IC) behaviour rate by model over all tasks and variants ( n = 168 n=168 samples per model). Error bars show 95% Wilson confidence intervals over sample-level adjusted IC labels.

items.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
items.abstract: MASPO is a novel framework for jointly optimizing role-specific prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems. Its core innovation is a joint evaluation mechanism that assesses prompts based on their contribution to downstream agent success, bridging local and global objectives without needing ground-truth labels. This is efficiently achieved through a data-driven evolutionary beam search, leading to improved system-level performance.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the MASPO Framework. The optimization proceeds sequentially following the topological order of the agent graph (Top-Right). (Top) For a specific target agent, the Prompt Optimizer analyzes execution traces (context 𝒞 \( \mathcal{C} \) and output o o ) from sampled batches ℬ i ​ t ​ e ​ r ∪ ℬ m ​ i ​ s \( \mathcal{B}_{iter} \)\( \cup \)\( \mathcal{B}_{mis} \) to generate candidate prompts 𝒫 c ​ a ​ n ​ d \( \mathcal{P}_{cand} \) . These candidates are rigorously assessed by the LLM Evaluator across three distinct dimensions: local adherence, lookahead potential, and global alignment. (Bottom-Left) To resolve credit assignment, we synthesize these evaluations into a Joint Reward Model. Crucially, we identify and mine Misalignment Cases to explicitly guide the optimizer towards repairing coordination breakdowns. (Bottom-Right) Navigating the high-dimensional search space, the framework employs a Trace-Guided Beam Search. This mechanism maintains a beam of Top-K candidates, accumulating joint reward scores along the path to iteratively evolve and select the optimal prompt.

items.title: NeuroAgent: LLM Agents for Multimodal Neuroimaging Analysis and Research
items.abstract: NeuroAgent is an LLM-driven agentic framework designed to automate complex, multimodal neuroimaging analysis workflows, spanning preprocessing to downstream tasks. It utilizes a hierarchical multi-agent architecture with a Generate-Execute-Validate engine to autonomously create, run, and debug executable code for various imaging modalities (sMRI, fMRI, dMRI, PET). The core contribution is streamlining the path from raw data to reproducible scientific results via intelligent automation and natural-language interaction.
items.figure_caption: NeuroAgent Framework Overview. The system comprises a Central Orchestrator (planning), Specialized Modality Agents (execution), and a Feedback-Driven “Generate-Execute-Validate” engine that enables reflective self-correction. A Human-in-the-Loop interface allows researchers to supervise and intervene at critical decision points.

items.title: PACZero: PAC-Private Fine-Tuning of Language Models via Sign Quantization
items.abstract: PACZero introduces a novel, highly private fine-tuning method for language models based on **PAC (Probably Approximately Correct) Privacy**, specifically targeting membership inference attack (MIA) resistance. The core method involves **sign-quantizing subset-aggregated zeroth-order gradients**, exploiting the resulting frequent "unanimity" steps where the update direction is agreed upon, thus incurring zero conditional mutual information cost. This approach achieves a strong privacy guarantee—matching MIA posterior success at the prior—that surpasses standard Differential Privacy (DP) except at the extreme $\varepsilon=0$ setting.

items.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
items.abstract: Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO) is a reinforcement learning method for training agents capable of recursively spawning and delegating sub-tasks to new instances of themselves. This recursive structure enables inference-time scaling via divide-and-conquer, allowing agents to handle longer contexts and more complex problems. RAO's contribution is teaching agents the optimal strategy for delegation and communication, leading to improved training efficiency, context generalization, and reduced wall-clock time.

items.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents
items.abstract: SkillOS introduces a reinforcement learning (RL) framework for self-evolving agents to automatically curate a library of reusable skills from experience. It pairs a frozen agent executor with a trainable skill curator that updates an external SkillRepo using composite rewards derived from grouped task streams. This method addresses the bottleneck of skill curation by learning long-term curation policies from indirect feedback, enabling agents to self-improve over time.
items.figure_caption: SkillOS pairs a frozen Agent Executor with a trainable Skill Curator . The executor retrieves relevant skills from SkillRepo to act; the curator edits the repo (insert/update/delete) based on the resulting experiences, with Markdown as the skill format.

items.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
items.abstract: StraTA introduces an explicit, sampled trajectory-level strategy to agentic reinforcement learning, addressing the limitations of purely reactive LLM agents in long-horizon tasks. It jointly trains a strategy generator and action execution policy using a hierarchical rollout design, enhanced by diverse strategy sampling and self-judgment. This method significantly improves both sample efficiency and final performance across complex ALFWorld, WebShop, and SciWorld benchmarks.

items.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
items.abstract: The paper introduces the Superintelligent Retrieval Agent (SIRA), which aims to overcome the inefficiency of multi-round exploratory search in retrieval-augmented agents. SIRA defines "superintelligence" as compressing iterative search into a single, corpus-discriminative retrieval action by identifying terms that best separate desired evidence from irrelevant corpus noise. This is achieved by using an LLM to enrich documents offline with necessary search vocabulary.
items.figure_caption: Three retrieval paradigms compared. (a) Dense retrieval encodes queries and documents into a shared embedding space and performs nearest-neighbor search; the process is one-shot but opaque and requires in-domain supervision. (b) Multi-step agent retrieval uses an LLM to iteratively formulate queries, read retrieved passages, and reformulate over N N rounds; later queries benefit from accumulated retrieval context. (c) SIRA produces an expert-level retrieval action in a single shot: the LLM generates an expected-response sketch, validates proposed terms against corpus statistics, and compiles a controlled BM25 query with weighted keywords and constraints, all without reading any retrieved passages.

items.title: The Structural Origin of Attention Sink: Variance Discrepancy, Super Neurons, and Dimension Disparity
items.abstract: This paper provides a mechanistic explanation for the "attention sink" phenomenon in LLMs, tracing it to a variance discrepancy in the value aggregation process of self-attention. This discrepancy is amplified by dimension disparity caused by sparse down-projections in FFN super neurons, forcing the first token to become a structural anchor. The authors validate this causal chain through controlled interventions modifying attention masks and token representation variance.
items.figure_caption: Schematic Overview of the Attention Sink Mechanism. Value aggregation causes dimension-wise variance decay for subsequent tokens, while the first token acts as a high-variance outlier. This discrepancy is preserved by output projections, activating super neurons in FFNs. Subsequently, the channel-sparse down-projections induce dimension disparity, resulting in the attention sink.

items.title: UniSD: Towards a Unified Self-Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
items.abstract: UniSD proposes a unified framework to systematically study and improve self-distillation (SD) for autoregressive Large Language Models (LLMs). It integrates multiple complementary mechanisms—such as multi-teacher agreement and EMA stabilization—to enhance supervision reliability and training stability during the distillation process. The core contribution is demonstrating which specific components effectively drive performance gains in SD across various models and benchmarks.
items.figure_caption: Overview of UniSD, a unified framework for self-distillation in LLMs. UniSD integrates agreement, stabilization, clipping, contrastive learning, and feature matching to enable systematic analysis. UniSD ∗ further integrates various components to improve LLMs without stronger external teachers.

items.title: Agentic AIs Are the Missing Paradigm for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Foundation Models
items.abstract: This paper argues that the prevailing model-centric approach is insufficient for handling Out-of-Distribution (OOD) generalization in Foundation Models (FMs) operating in open-world settings. The authors propose that **agentic AI systems** represent the necessary missing paradigm to address these structurally distinct OOD challenges. They formalize OOD in a stage-aware manner and demonstrate a fundamental parameter coverage ceiling for purely model-centric methods.
items.figure_caption: Three paradigms of OOD generalization for foundation models. Training-time and test-time model-centric methods both adjust the model. Agentic methods keep the model fixed and wrap it in a perceive–reason–act–verify loop with strategies including retrieval, tools, decomposition, verification, and abstention. The paradigms overlap on inference-time model adjustment but each contains actions outside the others’ reach.

items.title: Crafting Reversible SFT Behaviors in Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces a method to **mechanistically isolate and control Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) behaviors** within Large Language Models by compressing them into sparse, necessary subnetworks called "carriers." The core technique, **Loss-Constrained Dual Descent (LCDD)**, jointly optimizes model weights and routing masks under a utility budget to create these carriers. This allows for **inference-time control** of the learned behavior using the **SFT-Eraser** soft prompt, offering a causal alternative to post-hoc attribution methods.
items.figure_caption: An overview of the LCDD + SFT-Eraser pipeline. Stage 1 : standard SFT distributes the induced behavior broadly across model parameters (red), followed by LCDD that compresses the SFT-induced behavior into a sparse carrier. Components outside the carrier are reduced to their base-model state by construction (blue). Stage 2 : SFT-Eraser optimizes a soft trigger. Under normal inference without the trigger, the carrier preserves SFT behavior; while with the trigger, carrier activations are driven toward the base model.

items.title: Efficient Serving for Dynamic Agent Workflows with Prediction-based KV-Cache Management
items.abstract: This paper introduces PBKV, a novel system for efficient serving of dynamic LLM-based agent workflows. PBKV predicts future agent invocations within a workflow by fusing historical data and current context. Based on these predictions, it strategically manages the KV-Cache, prioritizing the retention of cache entries with high reuse potential in GPU memory to optimize serving efficiency.
items.figure_caption: A call graph for the code-generation task. The Tester conditionally triggers a retry path through Analyzer and Coder, i.e., a retry loop.

items.title: How Many Iterations to Jailbreak? Dynamic Budget Allocation for Multi-Turn LLM Evaluation
items.abstract: This paper introduces **DAPRO (Dynamic Allocation via PRojected Optimization)**, the first theoretically valid framework for dynamic budget allocation in multi-turn LLM evaluations. DAPRO efficiently allocates computational resources across sequential interactions to reliably bound the number of iterations required to observe rare events like jailbreaks. Its core contribution is providing distribution-free, finite-sample coverage guarantees while respecting the overall computational budget, overcoming the inefficiency of prior static allocation methods.
items.figure_caption: Illustration of our framework: (i) collecting data via dynamic budget allocation; (ii) calibrating a pre-trained model; and (iii) deploying the model at inference time to serve as a guardrail. 2 2 2 This figure was generated using Google Gemini based on a prompt designed by the authors and subsequently refined.

items.title: MARBLE: Multi-Aspect Reward Balance for Diffusion RL
items.abstract: MARBLE addresses the challenge of jointly optimizing multiple, often conflicting, reward dimensions in diffusion model reinforcement learning fine-tuning. It overcomes the limitations of weighted-sum aggregation, which suffers from sample-level mismatch, by introducing a novel method for balancing these multi-aspect rewards. The core contribution is a unified framework that allows for simultaneous, effective training across all desired criteria without manual stage scheduling.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of multi-reward training paradigms. Left: Training one model per reward requires maintaining multiple models and cannot generalize across reward dimensions. Middle: Sequential multi-reward training produces a single model but demands extensive hyperparameter tuning and handcrafted stage schedules. Right: Marble trains a single model on all rewards simultaneously with minimal manual effort.

items.title: Algospeak, Hiding in the Open: The Trade-off Between Legible Meaning and Detection Avoidance
items.abstract: This paper formalizes the trade-off in "Algospeak" strategies, where increased linguistic evasion simultaneously reduces detectability by moderation systems and understandability for human recipients. The authors introduce the concept of Majority Understandable Modulation (MUM) to define the point where evasion efforts begin sacrificing comprehension. They contribute a reproducible framework to generate meaning-preserving, tunable Algospeak variants to empirically study this dynamic.

items.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, reproducible framework using an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) parser to systematically extract and evaluate inline source citations from LLM-generated research reports. The core contribution is closing the verification loop by retrieving the actual cited content to assess citations across three dimensions: URL accessibility, topical relevance, and factual accuracy against the source. This allows for scalable, rigorous evaluation of source attribution in LLM research agents.
items.figure_caption: Source attribution evaluation framework. A deep research agent generates Markdown reports with inline citations, which are parsed via a Markdown AST parser to extract citation-claim pairs. Each pair is evaluated on Link Works (URL accessibility), Relevant Content (topical alignment), and Fact Check (factual accuracy).

items.title: Efficient Pre-Training with Token Superposition
items.abstract: The paper introduces Token-Superposition Training (TST), a simple, drop-in method to significantly boost data throughput during Large Language Model pre-training without altering the model architecture or training setup. TST achieves this via a two-phase approach: first, efficiently training on "bags" of combined tokens using a multi-hot cross-entropy objective, followed by a standard recovery phase. This method consistently improves performance and efficiency over baseline training across various model scales.

items.title: AI slop is killing online communities
items.summary: The proliferation of low-quality, AI-generated content ("AI slop") is degrading the value and engagement within online communities.

items.title: Motherboard sales 'collapse' amid unprecedented shortages fueled by AI
items.summary: Motherboard sales are collapsing by over 25% as chipmakers prioritize AI chip production, severely impacting PC component suppliers like ASUS, Gigabyte, and MSI.

items.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos and "almost no false positives"
items.summary: Mozilla reported that its new security analysis tool, Mythos, successfully identified 271 vulnerabilities with very few false positives.

items.title: Two Home Affairs officials suspended after AI 'hallucinations' found
items.summary: Two Home Affairs officials were suspended after it was discovered they used an AI system that produced "hallucinations" or false information.
The `anthropics/financial-services` repository likely provides **tools, code, or examples related to applying Anthropic's AI models to solve problems within the financial services industry.**

items.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
items.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

items.description: DFlash: Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding
items.summary: DFlash implements block diffusion to enhance Flash Speculative Decoding, aiming to improve decoding speed and efficiency.

items.description: Unlimited FREE AI coding. Connect Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Antigravity to FREE Claude/GPT/Gemini via 40+ providers. Auto-fallback, RTK -40% tokens, never hit limits.
items.summary: 9router is a free AI coding tool that connects various AI models like Claude Code and Copilot to multiple providers, featuring auto-fallback and token reduction to prevent hitting usage limits.

items.description: Stealth Chromium that passes every bot detection test. Drop-in Playwright replacement with source-level fingerprint patches. 30/30 tests passed.
items.summary: CloakBrowser is a stealth-focused Chromium browser, functioning as a drop-in Playwright replacement, featuring source-level fingerprint patches to successfully bypass all bot detection tests.

items.description: AI-Driven Life Cycle (AI-DLC) adaptive workflow steering rules for AI coding agents
items.summary: This repository provides adaptive workflow steering rules, powered by AI, to guide the life cycle management of AI coding agents.
AI-Trader is a fully automated, agent-native trading system designed to execute trades autonomously.

items.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
items.summary: This repository provides a local and encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Qwen, Llama.cpp, Ollama) and supports over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.

DAPRO efficiently allocates computational resources across sequential interactions to reliably bound the number of iterations required to observe rare events like jailbreaks. Its core contribution is providing distribution-free, finite-sample coverage guarantees while respecting the overall computational budget, overcoming the inefficiency of prior static allocation methods.
arxiv.figure_caption: Illustration of our framework: (i) collecting data via dynamic budget allocation; (ii) calibrating a pre-trained model; and (iii) deploying the model at inference time to serve as a guardrail. 2 2 2 This figure was generated using Google Gemini based on a prompt designed by the authors and subsequently refined.

arxiv.title: MARBLE: Multi-Aspect Reward Balance for Diffusion RL
arxiv.abstract: MARBLE addresses the challenge of jointly optimizing multiple, often conflicting, reward dimensions in diffusion model reinforcement learning fine-tuning. It overcomes the limitations of weighted-sum aggregation, which suffers from sample-level mismatch, by introducing a novel method for balancing these multi-aspect rewards. The core contribution is a unified framework that allows for simultaneous, effective training across all desired criteria without manual stage scheduling.
arxiv.figure_caption: Comparison of multi-reward training paradigms. Left: Training one model per reward requires maintaining multiple models and cannot generalize across reward dimensions. Middle: Sequential multi-reward training produces a single model but demands extensive hyperparameter tuning and handcrafted stage schedules. Right: Marble trains a single model on all rewards simultaneously with minimal manual effort.

arxiv.title: Algospeak, Hiding in the Open: The Trade-off Between Legible Meaning and Detection Avoidance
arxiv.abstract: This paper formalizes the trade-off in "Algospeak" strategies, where increased linguistic evasion simultaneously reduces detectability by moderation systems and understandability for human recipients. The authors introduce the concept of Majority Understandable Modulation (MUM) to define the point where evasion efforts begin sacrificing comprehension. They contribute a reproducible framework to generate meaning-preserving, tunable Algospeak variants to empirically study this dynamic.

arxiv.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, reproducible framework using an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) parser to systematically extract and evaluate inline source citations from LLM-generated research reports. The core contribution is closing the verification loop by retrieving the actual cited content to assess citations across three dimensions: URL accessibility, topical relevance, and factual accuracy against the source. This allows for scalable, rigorous evaluation of source attribution in LLM research agents.
arxiv.figure_caption: Source attribution evaluation framework. A deep research agent generates Markdown reports with inline citations, which are parsed via a Markdown AST parser to extract citation-claim pairs. Each pair is evaluated on Link Works (URL accessibility), Relevant Content (topical alignment), and Fact Check (factual accuracy).

arxiv.title: Efficient Pre-Training with Token Superposition
arxiv.abstract: The paper introduces Token-Superposition Training (TST), a simple, drop-in method to significantly boost data throughput during Large Language Model pre-training without altering the model architecture or training setup. TST achieves this via a two-phase approach: first, efficiently training on "bags" of combined tokens using a multi-hot cross-entropy objective, followed by a standard recovery phase. This method consistently improves performance and efficiency over baseline training across various model scales.

papers.title: Can RL Teach Long-Horizon Reasoning to LLMs? Expressiveness Is Key
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **ScaleLogic**, a synthetic framework to systematically study how Reinforcement Learning (RL) improves LLM reasoning across varying **depth (horizon)** and **logical expressiveness**. The core contribution is demonstrating that the required RL training compute scales with reasoning depth via a power law ($T \propto D^γ$), where the scaling exponent ($\gamma$) increases significantly as the underlying logic becomes more expressive (e.g., adding "and," "or," "not," and "for all").
papers.figure_caption: Overview of ScaleLogic . Each problem has B B candidate proof trees, exactly one of which has a provable conclusion; the others are made unprovable by corrupting one axiom. The depth D D controls proof depth. Left: Implication-only reasoning. Right: The most expressive logic setting (referred to as + Quantification in Section 3.2 ) combines conjunction, disjunction, negation, and universal quantification.

papers.title: Continuous Latent Diffusion Language Model
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Cola DLM, a hierarchical latent diffusion language model that separates global semantic organization from local textual realization. It uses a Text VAE for text-to-latent mapping and a block-causal DiT to model a global semantic prior in continuous latent space. This non-autoregressive approach improves generation efficiency and semantic modeling by performing latent prior transport during the diffusion process.
papers.figure_caption: The Overall Workflow of Cola DLM. Detailed illustration of the training and inference pipeline of 𝒞 ​ o ​ l ​ a \( \mathcal{C} \)ola DLM . Training Stage 1 shows Text VAE pretraining with reconstruction, BERT, and KL losses. Training Stage 2 shows joint pretraining of the Text VAE and Text DiT with gradient control for stable optimization, where a specialized block-causal mechanism is adopted in the DiT. Inference Stage illustrates the decoding process with KV cache.

papers.title: Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, low-stakes benchmark to measure the propensity of LLM agents to engage in instrumental convergence (IC) behaviors, such as self-preservation, which might lead to instruction violation. The benchmark comprises seven tasks, each featuring a policy-violating shortcut, and an accompanying framework to test how varying factors influence this behavior. The core contribution is a realistic evaluation method to quantify the risk of AI agents pursuing useful but unintended instrumental goals.
papers.figure_caption: Aggregate adjusted instrumental-convergence (IC) behaviour rate by model over all tasks and variants ( n = 168 n=168 samples per model). Error bars show 95% Wilson confidence intervals over sample-level adjusted IC labels.

papers.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
papers.abstract: MASPO is a novel framework for jointly optimizing role-specific prompts in LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems. Its core innovation is a joint evaluation mechanism that assesses prompts based on their contribution to downstream agent success, bridging local and global objectives without needing ground-truth labels. This is efficiently achieved through a data-driven evolutionary beam search, leading to improved system-level performance.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the MASPO Framework. The optimization proceeds sequentially following the topological order of the agent graph (Top-Right). (Top) For a specific target agent, the Prompt Optimizer analyzes execution traces (context 𝒞 \( \mathcal{C} \) and output o o ) from sampled batches ℬ i ​ t ​ e ​ r ∪ ℬ m ​ i ​ s \( \mathcal{B}_{iter} \)\( \cup \)\( \mathcal{B}_{mis} \) to generate candidate prompts 𝒫 c ​ a ​ n ​ d \( \mathcal{P}_{cand} \) . These candidates are rigorously assessed by the LLM Evaluator across three distinct dimensions: local adherence, lookahead potential, and global alignment. (Bottom-Left) To resolve credit assignment, we synthesize these evaluations into a Joint Reward Model. Crucially, we identify and mine Misalignment Cases to explicitly guide the optimizer towards repairing coordination breakdowns. (Bottom-Right) Navigating the high-dimensional search space, the framework employs a Trace-Guided Beam Search. This mechanism maintains a beam of Top-K candidates, accumulating joint reward scores along the path to iteratively evolve and select the optimal prompt.

papers.title: NeuroAgent: LLM Agents for Multimodal Neuroimaging Analysis and Research
papers.abstract: NeuroAgent is an LLM-driven agentic framework designed to automate complex, multimodal neuroimaging analysis workflows, spanning preprocessing to downstream tasks. It utilizes a hierarchical multi-agent architecture with a Generate-Execute-Validate engine to autonomously create, run, and debug executable code for various imaging modalities (sMRI, fMRI, dMRI, PET). The core contribution is streamlining the path from raw data to reproducible scientific results via intelligent automation and natural-language interaction.
papers.figure_caption: NeuroAgent Framework Overview. The system comprises a Central Orchestrator (planning), Specialized Modality Agents (execution), and a Feedback-Driven “Generate-Execute-Validate” engine that enables reflective self-correction. A Human-in-the-Loop interface allows researchers to supervise and intervene at critical decision points.

papers.title: PACZero: PAC-Private Fine-Tuning of Language Models via Sign Quantization
papers.abstract: PACZero introduces a novel, highly private fine-tuning method for language models based on **PAC (Probably Approximately Correct) Privacy**, specifically targeting membership inference attack (MIA) resistance. The core method involves **sign-quantizing subset-aggregated zeroth-order gradients**, exploiting the resulting frequent "unanimity" steps where the update direction is agreed upon, thus incurring zero conditional mutual information cost. This approach achieves a strong privacy guarantee—matching MIA posterior success at the prior—that surpasses standard Differential Privacy (DP) except at the extreme $\varepsilon=0$ setting.

papers.title: Recursive Agent Optimization
papers.abstract: Recursive Agent Optimization (RAO) is a reinforcement learning method for training agents capable of recursively spawning and delegating sub-tasks to new instances of themselves. This recursive structure enables inference-time scaling via divide-and-conquer, allowing agents to handle longer contexts and more complex problems. RAO's contribution is teaching agents the optimal strategy for delegation and communication, leading to improved training efficiency, context generalization, and reduced wall-clock time.

papers.title: SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents
papers.abstract: SkillOS introduces a reinforcement learning (RL) framework for self-evolving agents to automatically curate a library of reusable skills from experience. It pairs a frozen agent executor with a trainable skill curator that updates an external SkillRepo using composite rewards derived from grouped task streams. This method addresses the bottleneck of skill curation by learning long-term curation policies from indirect feedback, enabling agents to self-improve over time.
papers.figure_caption: SkillOS pairs a frozen Agent Executor with a trainable Skill Curator . The executor retrieves relevant skills from SkillRepo to act; the curator edits the repo (insert/update/delete) based on the resulting experiences, with Markdown as the skill format.

papers.title: StraTA: Incentivizing Agentic Reinforcement Learning with Strategic Trajectory Abstraction
papers.abstract: StraTA introduces an explicit, sampled trajectory-level strategy to agentic reinforcement learning, addressing the limitations of purely reactive LLM agents in long-horizon tasks. It jointly trains a strategy generator and action execution policy using a hierarchical rollout design, enhanced by diverse strategy sampling and self-judgment. This method significantly improves both sample efficiency and final performance across complex ALFWorld, WebShop, and SciWorld benchmarks.

papers.title: Superintelligent Retrieval Agent: The Next Frontier of Information Retrieval
papers.abstract: The paper introduces the Superintelligent Retrieval Agent (SIRA), which aims to overcome the inefficiency of multi-round exploratory search in retrieval-augmented agents. SIRA defines "superintelligence" as compressing iterative search into a single, corpus-discriminative retrieval action by identifying terms that best separate desired evidence from irrelevant corpus noise. This is achieved by using an LLM to enrich documents offline with necessary search vocabulary.
papers.figure_caption: Three retrieval paradigms compared. (a) Dense retrieval encodes queries and documents into a shared embedding space and performs nearest-neighbor search; the process is one-shot but opaque and requires in-domain supervision. (b) Multi-step agent retrieval uses an LLM to iteratively formulate queries, read retrieved passages, and reformulate over N N rounds; later queries benefit from accumulated retrieval context. (c) SIRA produces an expert-level retrieval action in a single shot: the LLM generates an expected-response sketch, validates proposed terms against corpus statistics, and compiles a controlled BM25 query with weighted keywords and constraints, all without reading any retrieved passages.

papers.title: The Structural Origin of Attention Sink: Variance Discrepancy, Super Neurons, and Dimension Disparity
papers.abstract: This paper provides a mechanistic explanation for the "attention sink" phenomenon in LLMs, tracing it to a variance discrepancy in the value aggregation process of self-attention. This discrepancy is amplified by dimension disparity caused by sparse down-projections in FFN super neurons, forcing the first token to become a structural anchor. The authors validate this causal chain through controlled interventions modifying attention masks and token representation variance.
papers.figure_caption: Schematic Overview of the Attention Sink Mechanism. Value aggregation causes dimension-wise variance decay for subsequent tokens, while the first token acts as a high-variance outlier. This discrepancy is preserved by output projections, activating super neurons in FFNs. Subsequently, the channel-sparse down-projections induce dimension disparity, resulting in the attention sink.

papers.title: UniSD: Towards a Unified Self-Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
papers.abstract: UniSD proposes a unified framework to systematically study and improve self-distillation (SD) for autoregressive Large Language Models (LLMs). It integrates multiple complementary mechanisms—such as multi-teacher agreement and EMA stabilization—to enhance supervision reliability and training stability during the distillation process. The core contribution is demonstrating which specific components effectively drive performance gains in SD across various models and benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of UniSD, a unified framework for self-distillation in LLMs. UniSD integrates agreement, stabilization, clipping, contrastive learning, and feature matching to enable systematic analysis. UniSD ∗ further integrates various components to improve LLMs without stronger external teachers.

papers.title: Agentic AIs Are the Missing Paradigm for Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Foundation Models
papers.abstract: This paper argues that the prevailing model-centric approach is insufficient for handling Out-of-Distribution (OOD) generalization in Foundation Models (FMs) operating in open-world settings. The authors propose that **agentic AI systems** represent the necessary missing paradigm to address these structurally distinct OOD challenges. They formalize OOD in a stage-aware manner and demonstrate a fundamental parameter coverage ceiling for purely model-centric methods.
papers.figure_caption: Three paradigms of OOD generalization for foundation models. Training-time and test-time model-centric methods both adjust the model. Agentic methods keep the model fixed and wrap it in a perceive–reason–act–verify loop with strategies including retrieval, tools, decomposition, verification, and abstention. The paradigms overlap on inference-time model adjustment but each contains actions outside the others’ reach.

papers.title: Crafting Reversible SFT Behaviors in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a method to **mechanistically isolate and control Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) behaviors** within Large Language Models by compressing them into sparse, necessary subnetworks called "carriers." The core technique, **Loss-Constrained Dual Descent (LCDD)**, jointly optimizes model weights and routing masks under a utility budget to create these carriers. This allows for **inference-time control** of the learned behavior using the **SFT-Eraser** soft prompt, offering a causal alternative to post-hoc attribution methods.
papers.figure_caption: An overview of the LCDD + SFT-Eraser pipeline. Stage 1 : standard SFT distributes the induced behavior broadly across model parameters (red), followed by LCDD that compresses the SFT-induced behavior into a sparse carrier. Components outside the carrier are reduced to their base-model state by construction (blue). Stage 2 : SFT-Eraser optimizes a soft trigger. Under normal inference without the trigger, the carrier preserves SFT behavior; while with the trigger, carrier activations are driven toward the base model.

papers.title: Efficient Serving for Dynamic Agent Workflows with Prediction-based KV-Cache Management
papers.abstract: This paper introduces PBKV, a novel system for efficient serving of dynamic LLM-based agent workflows. PBKV predicts future agent invocations within a workflow by fusing historical data and current context. Based on these predictions, it strategically manages the KV-Cache, prioritizing the retention of cache entries with high reuse potential in GPU memory to optimize serving efficiency.
papers.figure_caption: A call graph for the code-generation task. The Tester conditionally triggers a retry path through Analyzer and Coder, i.e., a retry loop.

papers.title: How Many Iterations to Jailbreak? Dynamic Budget Allocation for Multi-Turn LLM Evaluation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **DAPRO (Dynamic Allocation via PRojected Optimization)**, the first theoretically valid framework for dynamic budget allocation in multi-turn LLM evaluations. DAPRO efficiently allocates computational resources across sequential interactions to reliably bound the number of iterations required to observe rare events like jailbreaks. Its core contribution is providing distribution-free, finite-sample coverage guarantees while respecting the overall computational budget, overcoming the inefficiency of prior static allocation methods.
papers.figure_caption: Illustration of our framework: (i) collecting data via dynamic budget allocation; (ii) calibrating a pre-trained model; and (iii) deploying the model at inference time to serve as a guardrail. 2 2 2 This figure was generated using Google Gemini based on a prompt designed by the authors and subsequently refined.

papers.title: MARBLE: Multi-Aspect Reward Balance for Diffusion RL
papers.abstract: MARBLE addresses the challenge of jointly optimizing multiple, often conflicting, reward dimensions in diffusion model reinforcement learning fine-tuning. It overcomes the limitations of weighted-sum aggregation, which suffers from sample-level mismatch, by introducing a novel method for balancing these multi-aspect rewards. The core contribution is a unified framework that allows for simultaneous, effective training across all desired criteria without manual stage scheduling.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of multi-reward training paradigms. Left: Training one model per reward requires maintaining multiple models and cannot generalize across reward dimensions. Middle: Sequential multi-reward training produces a single model but demands extensive hyperparameter tuning and handcrafted stage schedules. Right: Marble trains a single model on all rewards simultaneously with minimal manual effort.

papers.title: Algospeak, Hiding in the Open: The Trade-off Between Legible Meaning and Detection Avoidance
papers.abstract: This paper formalizes the trade-off in "Algospeak" strategies, where increased linguistic evasion simultaneously reduces detectability by moderation systems and understandability for human recipients. The authors introduce the concept of Majority Understandable Modulation (MUM) to define the point where evasion efforts begin sacrificing comprehension. They contribute a reproducible framework to generate meaning-preserving, tunable Algospeak variants to empirically study this dynamic.

papers.title: Cited but Not Verified: Parsing and Evaluating Source Attribution in LLM Deep Research Agents
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a novel, reproducible framework using an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) parser to systematically extract and evaluate inline source citations from LLM-generated research reports. The core contribution is closing the verification loop by retrieving the actual cited content to assess citations across three dimensions: URL accessibility, topical relevance, and factual accuracy against the source. This allows for scalable, rigorous evaluation of source attribution in LLM research agents.
papers.figure_caption: Source attribution evaluation framework. A deep research agent generates Markdown reports with inline citations, which are parsed via a Markdown AST parser to extract citation-claim pairs. Each pair is evaluated on Link Works (URL accessibility), Relevant Content (topical alignment), and Fact Check (factual accuracy).

papers.title: Efficient Pre-Training with Token Superposition
papers.abstract: The paper introduces Token-Superposition Training (TST), a simple, drop-in method to significantly boost data throughput during Large Language Model pre-training without altering the model architecture or training setup. TST achieves this via a two-phase approach: first, efficiently training on "bags" of combined tokens using a multi-hot cross-entropy objective, followed by a standard recovery phase. This method consistently improves performance and efficiency over baseline training across various model scales.

hacker_news.title: AI slop is killing online communities
hacker_news.summary: The proliferation of low-quality, AI-generated content ("AI slop") is degrading the value and engagement within online communities.

hacker_news.title: Motherboard sales 'collapse' amid unprecedented shortages fueled by AI
hacker_news.summary: Motherboard sales are collapsing by over 25% as chipmakers prioritize AI chip production, severely impacting PC component suppliers like ASUS, Gigabyte, and MSI.

hacker_news.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos and "almost no false positives"
hacker_news.summary: Mozilla reported that its new security analysis tool, Mythos, successfully identified 271 vulnerabilities with very few false positives.

hacker_news.title: Two Home Affairs officials suspended after AI 'hallucinations' found
hacker_news.summary: Two Home Affairs officials were suspended after it was discovered they used an AI system that produced "hallucinations" or false information.

github_trending.summary: The `anthropics/financial-services` repository likely provides **tools, code, or examples related to applying Anthropic's AI models to solve problems within the financial services industry.**

github_trending.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

github_trending.description: DFlash: Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding
github_trending.summary: DFlash implements block diffusion to enhance Flash Speculative Decoding, aiming to improve decoding speed and efficiency.

github_trending.description: Unlimited FREE AI coding. Connect Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Antigravity to FREE Claude/GPT/Gemini via 40+ providers. Auto-fallback, RTK -40% tokens, never hit limits.
github_trending.summary: 9router is a free AI coding tool that connects various AI models like Claude Code and Copilot to multiple providers, featuring auto-fallback and token reduction to prevent hitting usage limits.

github_trending.description: Stealth Chromium that passes every bot detection test. Drop-in Playwright replacement with source-level fingerprint patches. 30/30 tests passed.
github_trending.summary: CloakBrowser is a stealth-focused Chromium browser, functioning as a drop-in Playwright replacement, featuring source-level fingerprint patches to successfully bypass all bot detection tests.

github_trending.description: AI-Driven Life Cycle (AI-DLC) adaptive workflow steering rules for AI coding agents
github_trending.summary: This repository provides adaptive workflow steering rules, powered by AI, to guide the life cycle management of AI coding agents.
AI-Trader is a fully automated, agent-native trading system designed to execute trades autonomously.

github_trending.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a local and encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Qwen, Llama.cpp, Ollama) and supports over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.
